generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T ADELHEIDSQUELLE. "Mineral" Spring at Heilbrunn; contains among other substances, Iodine, Bromine, Alumina, Soda, etc. ANALYSIS (PETTENKOFER). Sod. Iod.: 0.22 Sod. Brom.: 0.867 Sod. Chlor.: 38.068 Sod. Sulph.: 0.048 Potass Chlor.: 0.020 Carbonate of Soda: 0.216 Carbonate of Lime: 0.584 Carbonate of Magnes.: 0.144 Carbonate of Iron: 0.072 Alumina: 0.142 Silicia: 0.147 Organic: 0.165 Free Carb. gas: 13.18 c.c. Carburetted H.: 8.02 Oxygen: 1.38 Nitrogen: 6.54 Authorities. Bethmann (Arch. 15). 1 , Man 61 years old, with chronic catarrh, took 20 ounces daily, fasting; 2 , Girl, 12 years old, well except a enlarged thyroid, drank three or four wineglasses daily after breakfast. MIND. ► Much trilling and tra-la-ing, which formerly she never did, [2] . HEAD. ► Confusion of head, better in open air, [1] . EYE. ► Eyes feel swollen, as after taking cold; without redness, [1] . NOSE. ► Toward the end of the cure, she had nose-bleed , and much tossing about the bed at night (comp. Iod.), [2] . ► Nose-bleed (the second day after beginning to take the waters the second time.), [2] . FACE. ► Cheeks became full, and the face red; was formerly thin and pale, [2] . MOUTH. ► Salivation, especially nights, [2] . STOMACH. ► Soon after commencing the water, appetite increased, became conversational and lively, [2] . ► Appetite for potatoes and sour fruit, [2] . [10.] ► Thirst unusual, [1] . ► Aversion to meat and roasted articles, [2] . ► Painful flatulent distension in pit of stomach, [1] . ABDOMEN. ► Stomachache in upper abdomen wakes him, [1] . ► Lower abdomen became thick and protruding, [2] . ► Copious flatus after gunpowder, [1] . STOOL AND ANUS. ► Diarrha, watery, clear brown, then yellow and watery, four or five times a day, with much offensive flatus; no pains, but loud rumblings, grumblings, and noises in the lower part of the abdomen, after every eating or drinking, especially after use of pears; good appetite (coffee, or coffee-drinking, relieved the attacks, especially the rumblings: Nat. carb . relieved the diarrha), [1] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Testicles sensitive, [1] . CHEST. [20.] ► Chest is tired; reading fatigues (comp. Nat. mur .), [1] . ► The whole chest is painfully sensitive, as if inflamed (Spt. nit. dulc., antidote), [1] . NECK AND BACK. ► The thyroid gland became much swollen, but not the cervical and inferior maxillary glands, [1] . UPPER EXTREMITIES. ► Small, hard indurations (large as a flaxseed) in the palm of the hand, very near the last joint of the fourth finger, adherent to the bone, [2] . LOWER EXTREMITIES. ► After rising in the morning, cramp in left calf twice disappeared after going around, [1] . SKIN. ► Hard pimples, with a red, inflamed areola, itching somewhat, with a yellow, pustular apex, somewhat moist on scratching it off (like small boils), on neck, chest, fingers, and toes (comp. Sil .), [1] . ( Note . This water has been used for hundreds of years for goitre, scrofulous troubles of various kinds, enlarged glands, etc.) Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
